_description: Model derivations for Colombia vs Ghana 2026-07-04 R32

rule_checks.RULE7: Borderline fires -- Ghana win=10.31%, just above the 10% Scenario-A floor but below the 15% RULE7 threshold. Treat as moderate favorite, not extreme blowout.
rule_checks.RULE15: Borderline -- Ghana win 10.31% is right at the Scenario A/B boundary. Not applying extra suppression beyond what real data already shows, since Ayew (0 shots/3 games) and Semenyo (0 SOT/3 games) already carry genuinely poor underlying production independent of any blowout-suppression logic.
rule_checks.RULE12: Flagged Q12 (offsides): own-team-avg lambda (7.0, driven partly by COL's 7-offside outlier vs CDR) vs match-environment lambda (4.5) diverge meaningfully. Even excluding the CDR outlier, COL's remaining games (3,3) still support an elevated rate. Blended and shaded down slightly from the raw own-avg reading (0.97) to 0.75 for outlier caution.
rule_checks.RULE9: Applied to James Rodríguez (Q2): market price (illiquid, 51%) trimmed for his 0G/0A group-stage record despite starting all 3 games.

espn_insight.note_on_ghana_forwards: Both Ayew and Semenyo are total blanks in the group stage -- 0 combined goals, 0 combined assists, 0 combined SOT across 6 player-games. Ghana's 2 group goals came from Yirenkyi and Luckassen, neither of whom is asked about in these 15 questions.
espn_insight.note_on_james: James Rodríguez started every game but has zero goal involvements -- a real signal worth trusting over the illiquid market's more generous 51% score-or-assist price.
